briefing.opening: Good morning. We will now begin the climate briefing for March 29, 2026.

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Expanding Domestic Carbon Neutrality Practices and Education
sections.content: Today, various community and institutional efforts for practicing carbon neutrality were prominent domestically. Suwon Special City is actively pursuing the establishment of an integrated carbon-neutral green city platform and an integrated energy control service, seeking to leap forward as a leading city [5, 9, 13, 15, 18, 30]. Gwanak-gu is strengthening the operation of 'Climate Growth School,' a carbon neutrality education program for future generations [7, 23, 28]. The Korea National Arboretum and Changwon Probation Office conducted education on climate change response and energy saving [1, 4], and the Ansan Environmental Foundation is actively fostering carbon neutrality practice leaders with a target year of 2026 [6]. In addition, a carbon-neutral environmental concert successfully concluded in Uiwang [8, 29], a pledge campaign by the Jeungpyeong Resource Circulation Citizen Center [11], tree-planting events in Gunpo City, Goheung, and Noan-myeon [16, 17, 22], efforts to promote green purchasing [19], and the groundbreaking of an eco-friendly complex facility in Gumi Industrial Complex [24] are among the active movements observed across the country for the transition to a carbon-neutral society.

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating Renewable Energy Transition and Current Challenges
sections.content: Various local governments across the country are implementing active support measures to expand the supply of renewable energy. Daegu City, Gwangju Metropolitan City, and Jeonbuk Self-Governing Province are promoting energy transition by implementing projects that support the installation costs of renewable energy facilities for households and homes [31, 32, 33, 38, 40]. Gyeonggi Province is contributing to the spread of renewable energy by promoting a model where fishing village residents become owners of solar power plants through its 'Sunshine Income Village' project [37, 39, 41]. However, alongside these positive developments, a regrettable accident occurred on the 26th in Yeongdeok-gun, Gyeongbuk Province, where three workers died due to a fire in an aging wind turbine, issuing a serious warning about the safety management and aging issues of renewable energy facilities [42].

sections.title: ⚠️ Intensifying Climate Threats and International Warnings
sections.content: Threats from climate change are intensifying both domestically and internationally, emphasizing the need for immediate action. Domestically, the South Chungcheong Provincial Council criticized the effectiveness of the '2045 Carbon Neutrality' policy, stating it 'focuses on outward appearance but lacks substance,' and urged for strengthening the policy's core content [12, 21]. Furthermore, concerns are rising about the impact of energy price volatility on the common people's economy, as the average gasoline price in Seoul surpassed 1,900 won per liter and is expected to soon enter the 2,000 won range [43]. Internationally, the impacts of climate change are severely manifesting across ecosystems, including threats to Amazonian dung beetles [45], new regional movements of alien plants [47], and a surge in Antarctic Ocean warming [50]. The socioeconomic damages of climate disasters are also becoming a reality, with Jamaican residents facing difficulties in reconstruction after Hurricane Melissa [54]. An Australian Senate report recommended actively confronting misinformation about climate change, emphasizing the importance of accurate information [51].

sections.title: 🌍 Domestic and International Cooperation and Research for Climate Change Response
sections.content: Domestic and international cooperation and new research efforts to address climate change are also continuing. Domestically, there is the Presidential Committee on Climate Crisis Response [2, 14, 25, 26, 27]. China and South Korea presented a blueprint for cooperation on climate change response at the Boao Forum, demonstrating their commitment to strengthening regional climate action [3]. Internationally, the Department of Environmental Protection (DEP) plans to hold climate dialogue workshops to hear community climate change concerns and ideas [48]. Changes in the global renewable energy market are being detected, such as the rapid growth of renewable energy generation and the anticipated golden age of ESS batteries in the U.S. during a potential second Trump administration [35]. Huawei also held a Global Installer Summit to discuss seizing renewable energy opportunities in the AI era [36]. Meanwhile, new research findings have revealed the intriguing fact that the length of a day is increasing due to climate change, once again highlighting the complexity of the climate system [53].

briefing.closing: As we have seen in today's briefing, while domestic and international efforts and cooperation to address climate change continue, intensifying threats and pressing challenges are also clearly emerging. This is a time that demands stronger and more tangible actions for a sustainable future. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.description: Despite being a renewable energy source that contributes to the local economy, successive accidents have heightened local residents' anxiety. There is a call for a shift from supply-focused policies to enhanced safety management. On the 26th, a fire at a wind turbine in Daebu-ri, Yeongdeok-gun, Gyeongbuk Province, resulted in the deaths of three workers inspecting the unit.

summary.phenomenon: On the 26th, a fire broke out in a wind turbine installed on a hillside in Daebu-ri, Yeongdeok-gun, Gyeongbuk Province, leading to the deaths of three workers who were inspecting the unit.
summary.outlook: Successive accidents have heightened local residents' anxiety, and there is a growing call for a shift from supply-focused policies to enhanced safety management.

articles.title: Seoul Gasoline Price Exceeds 1,900 Won…'Only Upward Factors Exist,' Expected to Reach 2,000 Won Soon
articles.original_title: 서울 휘발유 1900원 돌파…“상승 요인만 존재” 곧 2000원대 진입할 듯
articles.description: Seoul's average gasoline price exceeded 1,900 won per liter on the 29th, just three days after the second oil price cap took effect. This surge is attributed to accumulated oil price increase factors, including the deployment of U.S. ground troops. The rising fuel costs have also led to an increase in new electric vehicle registrations in February.

summary.phenomenon: On the 29th, Seoul's average gasoline price exceeded 1,900 won per liter, occurring just three days after the implementation of the second oil price cap.
summary.cause: Oil price increase factors, including the deployment of U.S. ground troops, are accumulating.
summary.outlook: Gasoline prices are projected to soon enter the 2,000 won range, and new electric vehicle registrations increased in February due to soaring fuel costs.

articles.description: Many say they have not received support to rebuild their homes months after the storm caused unprecedented destruction“Before Hurricane Melissa I could have navigated life, figured things out. But since its passage, everything has just been turned upside down,” said Kerry-Ann Vickers.Vickers was three months pregnant when Hurricane Melissa demolished parts of her home in the coastal town of Black River, in St Elizabeth, west Jamaica, last October. Nearly six months on, Vickers, 25, is still stru

summary.phenomenon: Last October, Hurricane Melissa caused unprecedented destruction in the coastal town of Black River, St. Elizabeth, western Jamaica. Nearly six months later, many residents still have not received support to rebuild their homes.
summary.outlook: Months after the hurricane, residents like Kerry-Ann Vickers continue to struggle with rebuilding their homes and lives, indicating ongoing hardship and a lack of support.
